PLAINTIFF PACIFIC PEJIU WU RESTAURANT PARTNERS, L.P.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO ISSUES 1, 2, 3 AND 4 BECAUSE CROSS-COMPLAINANT FAILED TO GIVE PLAINTIFF THE CONTRACTUALLY REQUIRED WRITTEN NOTICE AND OPPORTUNITY TO CURE. rulings.Case Title: ARIANNA MORAS et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 19, 2007, LINE 7. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION BASED ON GARRISON'S DECLARATION AND THE TESTIMONY REGARDING WHO WAS ON DUTY AS DESCRIBED IN THE DEPOSITIONS OF RAMARIZ, BUDNICK, HARRIS AND JONES. THERE ALSO ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARDING PRIOR INCIDENTS INVOLVING THE VICTIM. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS BASED ON FAILING TO TAKE PRECAUTIONS TO PROTECT PLAINTIFF AND FOR THAT REASON THE MOTION IS DENIED. OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE TO BE RULED ON AT THE HEARING. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTION TO PLAINTIFF'S SUR-REPLY IS GRANTED. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: CARL HANSEN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION Motion To Appoint Setsuko Hansen As Successor-In-Interest To, And Substitute Successor-In-Interest For, Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: PLAINTIFF CARL HANSEN ABD SETSUKO HANSEN'S MOTION TO APPOINT SETSUKO HANSEN AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO, AND SUBSTITUTE SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST FOR, DECEASED PLAINTIFF AND FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. APPEARANCES: GRANT WALTERS, ESQUIRE (BRAYTON PURCELL, 415.898.1555) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. THERE ARE NO OTHER APPEARANCES. RULING: COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COUNSEL WAIVES THE REPORTING OF THE MATTER. COURT LATER PLACES ITS RULING ON THE RECORD. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: DIANA PADILLA, CSR 9975

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA CORRECTIONAL PEACE OFFICERS' ASSOCIATIO VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitrator'S Opinion And Decision (C.C.P. 1285, Et Seq.: 1287.4)
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON THURSDAY, APRIL 19, 2007, LINE 17. PETITIONER CALIFORNIA CORRECTIONAL PEACE OFFICERS' ASSOCIATION'S Petition To Confirm Arbitrator'S Opinion And Decision (C.C.P. 1285, Et Seq.: 1287.4): THE AWARD IS AFFIRMED. THE ARBITRATOR DID NOT EXCEED HIS JURISDICTION ON WHEN INTERPRETING AND APPLYING THE TERMS OF THE M.O.U. THE REQUEST FOR A STAY IS DENIED. SIMILAR REQUESTS HAVE BEEN MADE REPEATEDLY AND HAVE BEEN DENIED. =(302/PJM/KD)
